# UC-19.3.10 · OpenStack Keystone Failed Authentication Burst Detection (Federation, Token Lifecycle, and Domain Boundary Crossings)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Security, Audit, Operational &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We guard the tenant identity gate where humans and robots trade scoped tickets before Nova or Neutron acts. When Keystone paths suddenly flood with refused proof tied to SAML glides, OIDC quirks, rotating Fernet keys, or messy scope swaps, we signal early while legitimate clouds still behave calmly.*

---

## Description

Raises an observability alarm when **Keystone v3** identity endpoints (**`/v3/auth/tokens`**, **`/v3/auth/projects`**, **`/v3/auth/domains`**) emit clustered **`401`/`403`** outcomes, **`Authorization failed`** strings, or **`keystone.exception.*`** faults tied to one client identity within sliding windows—surfacing bursts that align with **password spraying**, stale **application credentials**, **Fernet rotation** churn, **federation mapping** regressions (**SAML2** clock skew, **OIDC `aud`/`iss`** mismatch), **mapped federation** regex misses, **domain versus project versus system scope** confusion, **trust delegation** misuse, or orchestrator retries after **role assignment** churn.

## Value

Identity failures precede successful token reuse or lateral moves across tenants. Surfacing bursts early protects **Nova**, **Cinder**, **Neutron**, and **Heat** workflows from silent degradation while incident responders still have time to revoke **application credentials**, rotate **trust** chains, inspect **`policy.yaml`** enforcement, and coordinate **IdP** certificate updates—without waiting for operators to notice **`openstack token issue`** scripts failing across CI/CD.

## Implementation

Normalize **`X-Forwarded-For`** behind load balancers; maintain **`cidr_allow_automation`** lookups for Jenkins/GitLab egress; enrich **`client_ip`** with CMDB owner; schedule off-hours suppression windows separately from federation maintenance; pipe **`severity=critical`** rows into ticketing when **`fed_vals`** highlight federation regressions alongside diverse **`fail_bucket`** categories.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- OpenStack releases aligned with supported SLURP lines (**OpenStack 2024.1 Caracal**, **2024.2 Dalmatian**, or maintained downstream **RHOSP 17.x**, **Charmed OpenStack 2024.x**, **Mirantis**, **Wind River Cloud Platform**) running **Keystone v3** only.
- Splunk Universal Forwarder with **`inputs.conf`** monitors on **`**/var/log/keystone/keystone.log`**, **`/var/log/apache2/keystone-wsgi-public_access.log`** (path adapts for **`httpd`**/**`nginx`** layouts).
- **`Splunk_TA_nix`** (**Splunkbase 833**) if **`journald`** ingestion is required—match **`SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER`**/**`_SYSTEMD_UNIT`** to **`apache2.service`**, **`httpd.service`**, or **`keystone.service`**.
- Token cadence awareness: **`keystone-manage fernet_rotate`** default rotation (~**6h**) drives harmless renewal churn—your thresholds must tolerate rotation overlap windows separately from abusive bursts.
- Federation readiness: SAML2 **`AudienceRestriction`**, OIDC **`jwks_uri`** retrieval, and **`mod_auth_openidc`** redirect URIs documented with identity architects.
- Splunk roles granting **`openstack`** index access for responders.
- Lifecycle posture: **OpenStack Yoga** and older upstream trains sit outside today’s SLURP window—UUID-centric revocation semantics still surface bursts that resemble spraying unless you isolate estates by **`token.provider`** (**`uuid`** vs **`fernet`** vs **`jws`**).
- Ingress honesty: **`haproxy`**/`nginx`/**`Envoy`** terminate TLS upstream of **`keystone-wsgi-public`**—preserve **`Forwarded`**/**`X-Forwarded-For`**/**`proxy_protocol`** extraction before **`client_ip`** drives thresholds.
- Automation honesty: **`clouds.yaml`****/` ****`secure.yaml`** jobs (**`OSC`**/` `openstack`/` **`terraform-provider-openstack`**) legitimately **`POST`** **`/v3/auth/tokens`** during rotations—carry **`JOB_NAME`**/` **`PIPELINE_ID`**/` **`STACK_NAME`** enrichment where CI emits them adjacent to Keystone logs.
- CADF parity: **`audit`** middleware **`DSP0262`** payloads must retain **`observer`**/` **`initiator`**/` **`target`** JSON intact after **`oslo.messaging`** hops—truncating **`detail.reason`** destroys federation RCA.
- Capacity budgeting: **`Horizon`** login storms or **`Magnum`**/` **`Octavia`** orchestrators may inflate **`401`**/` **`403`** ratios during benign surge tests—baseline weekday variance before tightening **`failures`** gates.

### Step 1 — Configure ingestion and parsing parity
(1) **Create monitors** on each API node running `keystone-wsgi-public` (Apache **`HTTPD`** or **Nginx**/**`uWSGI`** pair listening on **`5000`** public and **`35357`** legacy admin paths).
(2) **Tag sourcetypes**: **`openstack:keystone`** for **`keystone.log`**, **`openstack:keystone:access`** for combined-style access logs containing verbs toward **`/v3/auth/*`**.
(3) **Optional CADF channel**: enable **`[audit] middleware_notifications = True`** in **`keystone.conf`** and relay **`audit`** notifications into **`openstack:keystone:audit`** JSON preserved verbatim.
(4) **Time sync**: federation bursts vanish when hypervisors drift—ensure **chrony/`systemd-timesyncd`** alarms tie back here.
(5) **Field extraction**: ship **`props.conf`/` `transforms.conf`** extracts that populate **`status`** codes from **`%{COMBINEDAPACHELOG}` macro placeholder patterns before SPL relies on **`rex`** fallbacks.
(6) **Multiline fidelity**: tune **`LINE_BREAKER`**/` **`TRUNCATE`**/` **`EVENT_BREAKER`** so chained **`Traceback`**/` **`HTTP`**/` **`sqlalchemy`**/` **`ldap`**/` **`sql`** fragments attached to one `401`/` `403` remain single events—otherwise **`stats`** fabricates bursts.
(7) **Index segregation**: route **`lab`**/` **`staging`** Keystone volumes separately when crews rehearse **`OS-FEDERATION`** mappings weekly.
(8) Sample proofs: stash scrubbed `head`/` `transaction` snippets proving `fed_hints`/` `fail_message`/` `scope_signal` distributions—golden-run comparisons shorten RCA.

### Step 2 — Author lookups and enrichment
(1) `cidr_allow_automation` CSV listing orchestrator subnets permitted to spike `401` during credential rotations.
(2) `keystone_service_accounts` listing `service` users plus `application credential` IDs exempt short-term.
(3) `idp_maintenance` calendar describing SAML/OIDC certificate rolls—reference `docs.openstack.org` federation chapters before blaming attackers.
(4) `policy_reload` timestamps whenever `policy.yaml` edits publish—pairs with bursts due to mismatched `system` vs `domain` scopes.
(5) `group_membership_churn` CSV keyed by `group_id`/` `domain_id`/` `timestamp` whenever `ldap`/` `posix`/` `freeipa` sync jobs widen/shrink memberships driving `409`/` `403` retries.
(6) `barbican`/` `designate`/` `ceilometer` adjacent lookups optional—those stacks mint `trust`/` `application credential` churn correlated via `request_id` hooks.

### Step 3 — Build the correlation search and alert
Embed the SPL from the `spl` field inside a scheduled alert (15m cron with 60s throttle). Pair with a companion `stats` search grouping by `fail_message` to distinguish federation (`fed_hints`) versus `application_credential` exhaustion.

Field rationale:
– `http_code` merges `rex` pulls from `COMBINED` logs with structured `status` fields.
– `fail_message` collapses Keystone `oslo_log` idioms (`Authorization failed`, `Could not find user`) into categorical buckets.
– `cadf_fail` catches `outcome:failure` audit payloads independent of HTTP wrappers.
– `scope_signal` surfaces `system` vs `domain` vs `project` confusion causing `403` storms.
– `token_family` separates `Fernet` churn vs `JWS` validation failures vs `UUID` revocation cascades.

Alert choreography: throttle `client_ip`/` `fed_hints`/` `reason_mix` tuples while permitting `severity=critical` overrides when `scope_signal` oscillates `system` vs `domain`/` `project` inside one `15m` bucket—signals `policy`/` `assignment` churn misclassified purely as brute-force attempts.
Companion hunts: overlay `successful` `POST` `201` `/v3/auth/tokens`/` `GET` `200` `X-Subject-Token` issuance searches keyed by `request_id`/` `HTTP` `Via`/` `correlation` IDs wherever `mod_headers`/` `uwsgi`/` `gunicorn` emit them.

### Step 4 — Validate against operator tooling
(1) Run `openstack token issue` from a jump host using `clouds.yaml` referencing the same `OS_AUTH_URL` as controllers.
(2) Compare `journalctl -u apache2` (`httpd`) timelines when bursts spike—confirm `WSGI` worker saturation vs genuine `401` ratios.
(3) Horizon: reproduce `401` responses via stale dashboard cookies while noting `CSRF` middleware differs—still validates UI-facing symptom overlap.
(4) Federation: inspect **`/v3/OS-FEDERATION/identity_providers/*/protocols/*` mapping JSON against `rules` regex expectations.
(5) Document `Heat` stacks separately—they rely on `trust` delegation that may reuse tokens without hitting `/v3/auth/tokens` repeatedly.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- `401` spikes immediately after `fernet_rotate` — validators briefly reject stale `X-Subject-Token` references until caches reconcile; widen `cidr` exemptions only for documented cron hosts.
- `fed_hints=saml` with `AudienceRestriction` mismatches — compare `SignatureMethod` algorithms against IdP metadata refresh cadence.
- `oidc` failures referencing `jwks_uri` retrieval errors — inspect `mod_auth_openidc` caches plus outbound firewall allowances.
- `mapped federation` `rules` mismatch — `regex` drift when upstream `eduPersonPrincipalName` formatting changed.
- `application_credential` bursts near expiry windows — correlate with CI/CD `rotate` jobs rotating `app_cred_secret` slower than `expiration` timestamps.
- `trust` delegation storms — `trustee` renewal loops after `Heat` stack `FAILED` states repeatedly `DELETE`/` `CREATE`.
- `403` clusters tied to `policy.yaml` reload — `oslo.policy` cached decisions stale until `keystone` restart.
- `409 Conflict` bursts — concurrent `role assignment` writes overlapping `group` expansion loops.
- `503` saturation disguised as auth failures — validate `WSGI` backlog metrics vs `nginx` `upstream` `timeout` counters.
- `UUID` legacy deployments — `revocation_events` backlog yields benign `401` clusters until `token` `flush` completes.
- `Splunk` ingestion duplicates multi-line `tracebacks` — collapse via `transaction`/` `dedup`** before thresholds inflate artificially.

Appendix — companion hunts (optional correlation)
Correlate severity=critical rows from this alert with OpenStackClient CLI failures (volume, compute, orchestration verbs) emitted under the same client_ip — those binaries often retry POST /v3/auth/tokens until service catalogs hydrate. When audit payloads arrive via RabbitMQ notifications.audit relays but keystone.log truncates Authorization failed detail lines, CADF reason.code or reason.reason_type subtrees inside openstack:keystone:audit events still justify paging federation SMEs whenever fed_hint spikes cluster with mapped federation failures unrelated to WAN password spraying.

Stretch goal — stretch-cluster rehearsals that bounce identity traffic across AZ-local VIPs may transiently elevate duplicate POST attempts during DNS or anycast shifts; annotate maintenance feeds so Splunk responders distinguish rerouted automation from hostile scans.

## SPL

```spl
index=openstack (sourcetype="openstack:keystone" OR sourcetype="openstack:keystone:access" OR sourcetype="openstack:keystone:audit") earliest=-4h
| eval blob=lower(coalesce(_raw, message, cadf_payload, ""))
| rex field=_raw "(?i)\s(?P<http_status>[45][0-9]{2})\s"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?P<req_path>/v3/auth/(?:tokens|projects|domains)|/v3/OS-FEDERATION/[^\s\"]+)"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)keystone\.exception\.(?P<kexc>Unauthorized|Forbidden|UserDisabled|DomainNotFound|ProjectNotFound)"
| eval http_code=coalesce(http_status, status, http_status_code, "")
| eval fail_bucket=case(
    match(blob, "authorization failed"), "authorization_failed",
    match(blob, "could not find user"), "user_missing",
    match(blob, "requires authentication"), "auth_required",
    match(blob, "not authorized to perform"), "forbidden_action",
    match(blob, "could not find domain"), "domain_missing",
    match(blob, "userdisabled"), "user_disabled",
    isnotnull(kexc), lower(kexc),
    1=1, "other"
  )
| eval cadf_fail=if(match(blob, "\"outcome\"\\s*:\\s*\"failure\"") OR match(blob, "outcome.{0,80}failure"), 1, 0)
| eval is_bad=if(
    cadf_fail=1 OR http_code IN ("401","403","404","409","500","503")
    OR fail_bucket IN ("authorization_failed","user_missing","auth_required","forbidden_action","domain_missing","user_disabled"),
    1, 0)
| where is_bad=1
| eval client_ip=coalesce(src_ip, clientip, remote_addr, x_forwarded_for, "")
| rex field=blob "(?i)(?P<fed_hint>saml|openid|oidc|os-federation|mapped)"
| eval scope_hint=case(
    match(blob, "system.?scope|system_reader|system_admin"), "system",
    match(blob, "unscoped"), "unscoped",
    match(blob, "domain.?scope"), "domain",
    match(blob, "project.?scope"), "project",
    1=1, "unknown"
  )
| eval token_hint=case(
    match(blob, "fernet"), "fernet",
    match(blob, "(jws|json web signature)"), "jws",
    match(blob, "application.?credential|app_cred"), "app_cred",
    match(blob, "uuid.?token"), "uuid",
    1=1, "unspecified"
  )
| bin span=15m _time
| stats count as failures dc(req_path) as paths dc(fail_bucket) as reasons dc(scope_hint) as scopes dc(token_hint) as tokens values(fed_hint) as fed_vals max(len(blob)) as widest by _time client_ip
| where failures>=35 OR (failures>=15 AND paths>=3 AND reasons>=2)
| eval severity=case(failures>=100 OR paths>=5 OR scopes>=4, "critical", failures>=55, "high", 1=1, "medium")
| sort - failures
| head 200
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summaries=t count FROM datamodel=Authentication.Authentication WHERE nodename="Authentication" Authentication.action=failure BY Authentication.src Authentication.user Authentication.app earliest=-4h
| rename Authentication.* AS *
| stats sum(count) AS failures BY src user app
| where failures>=80
| eval note="Requires CIM-compliant Keystone tagging; prefer primary SPL on native sourcetypes until fields are normalized."
```

## Visualization

(1) **Heatmap**: **`fail_bucket`** × **`client_ip`** density across **`15m`** buckets. (2) **Stacked column**: **`fed_vals`** versus **`token_hint`** counts for federation regressions versus credential-family churn. (3) **Sequence timeline**: **`scope_hint`** transitions preceding bursts (**`system`** versus **`domain`** versus **`project`**). (4) **Table**: **`client_ip`**, **`failures`**, **`paths`**, **`reasons`**, **`scopes`**, **`severity`**, **`fed_vals`** with **`drilldown`** to **`_raw`**. (5) **Single-value**: rolling **`failures`** rate indexed against **`cidr_allow_automation`** exclusions.

## Known False Positives

**Fernet key rotation churn** — clustered **401** responses appear while **keystone-manage fernet_rotate** advances **/etc/keystone/fernet-keys** and memcached views disagree briefly on which family validates **X-Subject-Token** references; automation subnets tied to controllers dominate **client_ip**, not internet sweep ranges.

**Federated IdP signing certificate rollover** — SAML2 metadata swaps or OIDC **jwks_uri** endpoint churn emit **SignatureMethod**, **AudienceRestriction**, **aud**, **iss**, or signature-verification retries that mirror spraying absent coordinated password guesses.

**Application credential expiration inside CI/CD** — pipelines using **app_cred_id** plus **app_cred_secret** retry **POST /v3/auth/tokens** until Ansible Vault or **secure.yaml** secrets align—burst density tracks **JOB_NAME**, not lateral hops across domain boundaries.

**Token revocation cascade after role reassignment** — **assignment** API churn or LDAP **group** membership sync forces clients to discard stale **uuid** or **fernet** references and reauthenticate; denial counts spike without hostile **Authorization failed** intent yet coarse SPL resembles brute-force ladders.

**oslo.policy reload misalignment** — configuration pushes **policy.yaml** faster than **keystone-wsgi** worker caches reconcile **system** versus **domain** versus **project** enforcement—**403** ratios climb while operators experiment with **system_scope** privileges and assignment backends briefly disagree.
